title: streblus glaber subsp. urophyllus
intro: Streblus glaber subsp. urophyllus, also known as the Indian liver plant or varnish tree, is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its small, oval-shaped leaves and unique ability to produce a resinous sap. The medicinal properties of Streblus glaber subsp. urophyllus include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. This plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and is also believed to have antioxidant and hepatoprotective properties. In traditional medicine, Streblus glaber subsp. urophyllus is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or resin, which can be taken orally to reap its health benefits.
